name: The Pasarelas del Vero - circular from Alquézar
name_original: Las Pasarelas del Vero – circular desde Alquézar
region_intro: Hiking around Alquézar offers exploration within the Sierra y Cañones de Guara Natural Park, a region characterized by dramatic canyons, impressive rock formations, and the sculpted landscapes of the Vero River. The area features diverse terrain, from river gorges to ancient pathways, providing varied hiking experiences. Its unique geological setting, shaped by water and wind, presents a network of trails through limestone cliffs and Mediterranean vegetation. This natural environment is home to significant biodiversity and historical sites, including prehistoric cave paintings. Best hiking trails around Alquézar The most popular hiking route is Somontano de Barbastro Natural Trail, a 6.2 miles (10.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 59 minutes to complete. This route explores the natural landscapes of the Somontano region. Another top favourite among local hikers is The Pasarelas del Vero - circular from Alquézar, an easy 2.5 miles (4.1 km) path. This trail features metal walkways anchored to rock faces, offering panoramic views over the Vero canyon. Local hikers also love the Alquézar: Basacol Pools and Quizans Shelter Trail, a 5.4 miles (8.8 km) trail leading through varied terrain including ancient pools and shelters, often completed in about 2 hours 49 minutes. Hiking around Alquézar is defined by dramatic river canyons, unique rock formations, and the extensive network of trails within the Sierra y Cañones de Guara Natural Park. The region offers routes suitable for various ability levels, from accessible paths to more demanding trails. The routes in Alquézar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 400 reviews. More than 4000 hikers have used komoot to explore Alquézar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Alquézar?
A: Alquézar offers a wide range of hiking experiences, with over 30 routes available. These trails cater to various skill levels, including easy, moderate, and more challenging options within the Sierra y Cañones de Guara Natural Park.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Alquézar?
A: The terrain around Alquézar is diverse, characterized by dramatic river canyons, impressive rock formations, and sculpted landscapes carved by the Vero River. You'll find paths winding through limestone cliffs, Mediterranean vegetation, and ancient pathways. Some routes, like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e932340273/the-pasarelas-del-vero-circular-from-alquezar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Pasarelas del Vero&lt;/a&gt;, feature unique metal walkways anchored to rock faces.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hiking options in Alquézar?
A: Yes, Alquézar has several easy routes suitable for families. A popular choice is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e932340273/the-pasarelas-del-vero-circular-from-alquezar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Pasarelas del Vero - circular from Alquézar&lt;/a&gt;, which is 2.5 miles (4.1 km) long and features safe, suspended walkways over the Vero canyon, offering an immersive experience for all ages.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes near Alquézar?
A: Many trails around Alquézar are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An excellent example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e932340273/the-pasarelas-del-vero-circular-from-alquezar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Pasarelas del Vero - circular from Alquézar&lt;/a&gt;, which offers stunning views and a unique experience along the river.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while hiking?
A: Hiking in Alquézar offers access to spectacular natural features. You can explore the dramatic Río Vero Canyon, visit the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1208961" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quizans Shelter&lt;/a&gt;, or discover the picturesque Basacol Pools. The region is also known for its rich biodiversity, including various bird species like Griffon vultures and golden eagles, especially in the Sierra y Cañones de Guara Natural Park.

Q: What historical or cultural sites can I explore on a hike?
A: Alquézar is rich in history. Many routes lead to prehistoric cave paintings, recognized as a UNESCO World Heritage Site, such as those found at the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/975044" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Vero Rock Art Sites&lt;/a&gt;. The village itself is home to the Collegiate Church of Santa María la Mayor, offering incredible panoramic views of the surrounding landscape.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Alquézar?
A: The Sierra y Cañones de Guara Natural Park, where Alquézar is located, offers pleasant hiking conditions during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable, especially for longer routes. Winter offers a quieter experience, though some higher trails might be cooler.

Q: Are there opportunities for wild swimming along the hiking trails?
A: Yes, the region is known for its crystal-clear waters. While specific wild swimming spots are not always marked on hiking trails, the Río Vero and its tributaries offer natural pools. A notable nearby attraction is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/348950" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto de Bierge&lt;/a&gt;, a popular spot for swimming and enjoying the natural environment.

Q: Are there any permits required for hiking in the Sierra y Cañones de Guara Natural Park?
A: While most general hiking trails in the Sierra y Cañones de Guara Natural Park do not require specific permits, certain adventure activities like canyoning often do. It's always advisable to check with the local visitor center or the park's official website for the most up-to-date regulations, especially if you plan to explore sensitive areas or engage in specialized sports.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Alquézar?
A: The hiking trails around Alquézar are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 400 reviews. Hikers often praise the stunning canyon views, the unique experience of the Pasarelas del Vero, and the diverse landscapes that blend natural beauty with historical sites.

Q: Where can I park when hiking in Alquézar?
A: Alquézar, being a popular destination, has designated parking areas, typically located at the entrance to the village or near major trailheads. It's recommended to arrive early, especially during peak season, to secure a spot. Some routes, like those leading to the Pasarelas del Vero, often have parking facilities nearby.

Q: Is public transport available to Alquézar for hikers?
A: Public transport options to Alquézar can be limited, as it is a smaller village nestled within a natural park. While there might be bus services from larger towns in the Huesca province, these are often infrequent. Most visitors travel by car, which offers the most flexibility for accessing various trailheads around the region.
tips: A very nice church in the restaurant area.
A beautiful place within an excellent route.

The popularity of the town of Alquezar, considered one of the most beautiful towns in Spain, together with the beauty of this section of the Vero River Canyon (Route of the Vero footbridges), a wonder to pass by and admire the surroundings.

The hermitage of San Gregorio, is located in the upper part of the town, approximately one kilometer away, which constitutes a magnificent viewpoint over the medieval town of Alquézar and Guara Somontano; In addition to being a great place to have a picnic as it is equipped with benches and tables, it is located in the heart of the Somontano Nature Trail.
